name: artemisia vulgaris (mugwort)
effects: preclinical and narrative review sources describe broad pharmacologic potential across artemisia species
interactions: Potential additive effects with other CNS-active or uterine-stimulating herbs/medications are not well quantified.
safetyNotes: Species-level and preparation-level variability can materially change safety profile and constituent exposure.

sources.title: PubMed search: artemisia vulgaris (mugwort)
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Artemisia vulgaris (mugwort) is a traditional herb in Eurasian medicine with modern pharmacology discussions concentrated in broader Artemisia reviews.
dosage: No single modern therapeutic dosage standard was established in the promoted review source.
duration: No direct duration data. Contextual inference: Used in ritual, dreaming or folk medicine.. No direct mechanismofaction data. preparation: Common preparations include tea infusions and dried aerial parts, but constituent concentration is preparation-dependent.
region: Traditional use is documented across Europe and Asia, with later spread to North America.
traditionalUse: Traditional records describe digestive, menstrual, and ritual uses in multiple regions; these are traditional-use claims, not clinical proof.
